commit.message:
feat(setup): support configurable project status mappings (#70)

* feat(setup): support configurable project status mappings

Summary:
- add workflow config for claimable and write-side project status
  mappings
- inspect project statuses in setup and gate normalization behind
  explicit confirmation and safety checks
- update doctor, docs, and the spec for custom project status flows

Rationale:
- let Kairastra work with repositories whose GitHub Projects use
  non-canonical status names
- preserve existing behavior by default while avoiding unsafe rewrites
  of live projects during setup

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(review): address PR feedback on status mappings

Summary:
- preserve explicit empty claimable-state configuration and fix
  project status overview totals
- drive review handoff and blocked-issue suppression from workflow
  status config
- align canonical setup statuses and bootstrap helper field-name
  handling

Rationale:
- address the concrete PR review findings without changing the
  overall status-mapping design
- prevent blocked issues from re-entering the queue until an
  operator changes their tracker state

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/bootstrap_github_project.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add remote Docker bootstrap installer (#67)

* feat(docker): add remote bootstrap installer

Summary:
- add a rerunnable remote Docker installer for managed Kairastra installs
- document the SSH bootstrap flow for remote Docker hosts such as a Mac mini
- align the Docker env example with the current KAIRASTRA_* runtime contract

Rationale:
- make remote Docker deployment a single-script workflow instead of a manual repo setup
- keep generated operator config outside the managed checkout so upgrades are simpler
- remove stale env naming that no longer matches the compose and runtime configuration

Tests:
- cd rust && cargo fmt --check
- cd rust && cargo test
- cd rust && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh
- scripts/install-remote-docker.sh --help

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(docker): harden remote bootstrap installer

Summary:
- lock down remote bootstrap config file permissions
- validate user-provided git args and guard git clone against option injection
- clarify the README bootstrap flow with a pinned download example and fixed rerun commands

Rationale:
- address PR review feedback around secret exposure and git argument safety
- make the documented SSH install flow reproducible and consistent with the installed script path

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh
- scripts/install-remote-docker.sh --help
- cd rust && cargo fmt --check
- cd rust && cargo test
- cd rust && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(jumia): ticket-based OAuth handoff for mobile connection (#956)

* feat(jumia): ticket-based OAuth handoff for mobile Jumia connection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): apply CodeRabbit findings — logging, encoding, index strategy

- Add error logging in ticket, exchange, and callback routes
- Extract KNOWN_OAUTH_ERRORS to module-level constant in callback
- Encode ticketId in deep link URL (encodeURIComponent)
- Handle oauth_state update failure in connect handler
- Use 60 * 10 instead of 600 for cookie maxAge consistency
- Remove unused user_id from ticket select query
- Replace redundant PK-based index with (status, expires_at) + (created_at)
- Wrap request.json() in try/catch in exchange endpoint
- Add incomplete deep link feedback in mobile screen

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): apply CodeRabbit round 2 — atomic redemption, sanitization, index

- Make ticket redemption + oauth_state binding atomic (single UPDATE)
- Validate OAuth config BEFORE consuming ticket
- encodeURIComponent on ticket.id in authUrl
- Log upsertError before returning 500 in exchange
- Validate tokens.expires_in (fallback to 3600 if invalid)
- Sanitize error response in exchange catch block
- Validate ticketId cookie length in callback
- Add partial unique index on oauth_state WHERE NOT NULL
- Add DB insert failure test for ticket endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): apply CodeRabbit round 3 — TTL extension, fallback handling, FK indexes

- Extend ticket expires_at to 5min at redeem time (60s was too strict for Jumia login + 2FA)
- Return { success: false, incomplete: true } when only fallback shop created
- Add regression test for fallback shop path
- Add CSRF exemption comments (Bearer auth, not cookies)
- Add FK indexes on merchant_id and user_id for efficient CASCADE deletes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore(changelog): record scan safety fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased scan/status fix placeholder with the actual
  implementation hash

Rationale:
- release-note-worthy changes in this repo must carry the concrete short
  hash before handoff

Validation:
- not run; changelog hash update only

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(api): harden scan and simulator status

Summary:
- block Scan.scan/1 from probing a backend that is already owned by a
  live conflicting master session
- infer simulator backend identity from live transport info when the
  runtime was started without a stored backend, and make detached runs
  explicit with backend=nil
- tighten the simulator status contract and add regression coverage for
  backend conflicts, scan safety, and subscription shape

Rationale:
- a standalone scan must not mutate station addresses on the same
  backend a running master already owns
- the new machine-readable status surface should not silently lose
  transport identity or advertise the wrong subscriptions type

Validation:
- mix format
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/backend_test.exs test/ethercat/scan_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add opfs tmp manager

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: A privacy-focused web UI for LLMs (OpenAI-compatible APIs and Ollama) designed to run directly from a portable directory on your filesystem.

commit.message:
chore(changelog): record backend refactor hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased backend/status/scan refactor placeholder with
  the actual implementation commit hash

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy changes to carry the concrete
  short hash before handoff

Validation:
- not run; changelog hash update only

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(api): make backend and runtime roles explicit

Summary:
- add EtherCAT.Backend normalization plus first-class
  EtherCAT.Master.Status, EtherCAT.Simulator.Status, and
  EtherCAT.Scan.Result surfaces
- require explicit backend: startup for master and simulator, and
  update docs, fixtures, hardware helpers, and the capture task to
  use the new transport model
- add status and scan coverage, and migrate simulator and hardware
  support tests to the backend-aware API

Rationale:
- callers should not have to reconstruct whether the simulator,
  master, and observed bus are running from fragmented queries
- explicit backend attachment and topology-first scan results match
  the product model better than the old mixed transport options

Validation:
- mix format
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix 3.14t build (#174)

* Xfail free-threaded lock trace undercounts

* Fix free-threaded cooperative lock retries

* Preserve venv directories in make targets

* Increase timeout.

* Deduplicate repeated lock wait report events

* Fix bytecode semaphore retry loop

commit.message:
Publish Pages docs for the OAI RFsim + UE sim dashboard walkthrough

The Pages docs already described the runtime bridge and proof
boundaries, but reviewers still had to reconstruct the simulation
 dashboard path from multiple docs, issues, and artifact directories.
This change adds a dedicated walkthrough page, publishes the current
dashboard screenshot in the docs bundle, and wires the page into the
architecture navigation and cross-references so future issue-driven
refreshes have one place to edit.

Constraint: Published docs must keep simulation proof distinct from
Constraint: bounded-standards and real-lab proof tiers
Rejected: Fold the walkthrough into doc 09 only | harder to keep
Rejected: issue/PR evidence and media reviewable in one place
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Refresh the screenshot asset and issue/PR evidence links
Directive: together whenever dashboard proof media changes
Tested: git diff --check
Tested: npm run docs:build
Tested: Rendered VitePress page and published asset spot checks
Not-tested: Fresh runtime dashboard launch or new YON-101 media capture

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Surface repo-local OAI UE simulation proof in the dashboard

The repo-local split gNB plus OAI UE request set now carries a committed
observe entrypoint, simulation proof metadata, and dashboard coverage for
attach, registration, session, and ping outcomes. The dashboard also keys
focused runs by artifact identity instead of shared change ID so observe,
verify, and capture-artifacts entries for the same lane stay selectable.

This also checks in reviewer-facing screenshots so the PR can show the
operator path and explicitly mark the captured state as simulation proof,
not real-lab proof.

Constraint: Simulation evidence must remain visibly separate from live-lab claims
Constraint: PR media lives under a gitignored artifacts tree and must be staged intentionally
Rejected: Reuse change_id as the dashboard focus key | observe/verify/capture runs for one lane collide
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep run focus keyed by per-artifact identity whenever one lane can emit multiple phase artifacts
Tested: mix contract_ci
Tested: mix runtime_ci
Tested: bin/ran-dashboard plus curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/health
Tested: curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/dashboard | jq '.activity.recent_changes[] | select(.id=="chg-oai-du-ue-local-001") | {id, phase, protocol_state: .protocol_state.outcome_rows}'
Not-tested: Full live Docker RFsim bringup was not re-run after final staging in this handoff pass

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(admin): include admin merchant in platform analytics (#948)

* fix(admin): include admin merchant in platform analytics

All paid orders belong to the admin merchant (Ogabassey), so
excluding is_platform_admin from analytics resulted in empty
dashboard cards. Remove the app-level filter to match the
updated materialized views.

commit.message:
fix(mobile-admin): remove LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS from iOS hardening plugin (#950)

The xcode npm pbxproj parser cannot re-parse $() variable references
(like $(inherited) and $(SDKROOT)) after writeSync. This broke
downstream Expo mods like withEntitlementsPlist during prebuild.

Expo/RN prebuild already sets LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS correctly, so
the plugin should not modify it.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: Storybook設定の不要コメントを削除

Storybook preview に残っていたコメントアウト import を削除した。\n変更範囲を設定ファイルのノイズ除去に限定している。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: DatePickerの制御入力警告を解消

DatePickerの表示用TextFieldに no-op の onChange を追加し、\n見た目を変えずに controlled input の React 警告を解消した。\n他の未コミット設定変更はコミット対象に含めていない。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(checkout): remove per-route CSRF checks blocking guest storefront checkout (#949)

PR #809 added checkCsrfProtection() to 156 API routes, but incorrectly
included storefront guest-facing routes. Guest users don't have CSRF tokens,
so POST /api/shipping/quotes returns 403 — breaking checkout before orders
can be created. No orders have been recorded since March 18 (10 days).

The Origin-based CSRF middleware in proxy.ts already covers all routes at
the middleware layer, making these per-route checks redundant. Remove them
from 7 guest-facing routes: shipping/quotes, shipping/quote,
discount-codes/validate, forms/submit, storefront/customer, wishlist,
and reviews.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(BUB-150): extract shared session-linking helpers (#126)

Summary:
- add a shared session-linking helper module for sibling parent-link
  inference and task window extraction
- rewire Claude and OpenClaw adapters to inject runtime-specific transcript
  readers while reusing the shared logic
- add focused session-linking unit coverage and record the reusable pattern in
  progress tracking

Rationale:
- remove duplicated linking logic across the two adapters without changing
  runtime-specific missing-file behavior
- keep adapter isolation by centralizing the core algorithm while preserving
  runtime-owned I/O and error policy decisions

Tests:
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory test -- --run
  test/unit/session-linking.spec.ts test/unit/adapter.spec.ts
  test/unit/openclaw.adapter.spec.ts
- npm run check
- npm test
- npm run test:bdd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Stabilize and expand the Playwright e2e suite

Run Playwright against a dedicated smoke environment instead of reusing an already-running local app. Use an isolated smoke port and explicit smoke database path, seed the e2e user before startup, and keep the managed Playwright server path separate from intentionally external base URLs.

Expand coverage beyond the old login smoke check to cover dashboard rendering and graph toggles, diary create/edit/delete, pain create/edit/delete, settings preference persistence, and password change plus restoration. Exclude chatbot coverage for now because that flow is scheduled for removal.

Add cleanup-first test helpers so dummy e2e data is purged before and after each test run, and keep execution single-worker to avoid cross-test contamination while operating on the same dedicated account.

Closes #53

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add DU/CU runtime protocol-state panels to the dashboard

The dashboard snapshot now normalizes repo-local OAI DU/CU protocol
fields and bounded-standards protocol evidence into a documented
operator surface. The inspector replaces the single generic OAI card
with distinct simulation and standards sections, and the snapshot
contract plus architecture notes now carry the provenance needed to
explain each field and counter.

Constraint: Must keep repo-local simulation proof visually distinct from bounded-standards evidence
Constraint: No new dependencies for the dashboard surface or validation path
Rejected: Leave protocol proof as a generic token list | operators still need raw-log parsing and source hunting
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Extend protocol-state panels only from declared observe/verify artifact contracts so the UI does not invent protocol claims
Tested: mix test apps/ran_observability/test/ran_observability/dashboard_snapshot_test.exs; bin/ran-dashboard on http://127.0.0.1:4051 with RAN_TOPOLOGY_FILE=config/lab/topology.single_du.rfsim.json; curl /api/health and /api/dashboard; rendered screenshot artifacts/pr_media/yon-98/dashboard-protocol-state-tall.png
Not-tested: GitHub Actions/PR checks
Related: YON-98

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
